 {"id":"bd-icfe","title":"gt spawn/crew setup should create .beads/redirect for worktrees","description":"Crew clones and polecats need a .beads/redirect file pointing to the shared beads database (../../mayor/rig/.beads). Currently:

- redirect files can get deleted by git clean
- not auto-created during gt spawn or worktree setup
- missing redirects cause 'no beads database found' errors

Found missing in: gastown/joe, beads/zoey (after git clean)

Fix options:
1. gt spawn creates redirect during worktree setup
2. gt prime regenerates missing redirects
3. bd commands auto-detect worktree and find shared beads

This should be standard Gas Town rig configuration.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T01:30:26.115872-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:51:25.740811-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:51:25.740811-08:00","close_reason":"Moved to gastown: gt-b6qm"}

Makes molecules immortal.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T23:52:17.462882-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T00:07:25.803058-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T00:07:25.803058-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented bd mol run command","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-icnf","depends_on_id":"bd-ffjt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T23:52:25.871742-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ieyy","title":"bd close --continue: auto-advance to next molecule step","description":"Add --continue flag to bd close for seamless molecule step transitions.

## Usage

bd close <step-id> --continue [--no-auto]

## Behavior

1. Closes the specified step
2. Finds next ready step in same molecule (sibling/child)
3. By default, marks it in_progress (--no-auto to skip)
4. Outputs the transition

## Output

[done] Closed gt-abc.3: Implement feature

Next ready in molecule:
  gt-abc.4: Write tests

[arrow] Marked in_progress (use --no-auto to skip)

## If no next step

[done] Closed gt-abc.6: Exit decision

Molecule gt-abc complete! All steps closed.
Consider: bd mol squash gt-abc --summary '...'

## Key behaviors
- Detects parent molecule from closed step
- Finds next unblocked sibling
- Auto-claims by default (propulsion principle)
- Graceful handling when molecule is complete

## Gas Town integration
- gt-lz13: Update templates with nav workflow
- gt-um6q: Update docs with nav workflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:03:44.238243-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:36:31.937727-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T17:36:31.937727-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented mol current and close --continue"}

 {"id":"bd-iic1","title":"Phase 2.2: Switch bdt storage to TOON format","description":"Currently bdt stores issues in JSONL format in issues.toon file. Phase 2.2 must implement actual TOON format storage - this is the fundamental goal of the bdtoon project.

## Current State (Phase 2.1)
- issues.toon stores JSONL (intermediate format)
- --toon flag allows output in TOON format for LLM consumption
- Problem: We're not actually using TOON as the fundamental storage format

## Required Work (Phase 2.2)
1. Switch issue file I/O to write TOON format instead of JSONL
   - Update cmd/bdt/storage.go to use EncodeTOON for writing
   - Update cmd/bdt/storage.go to decode TOON (currently decodes JSON)
   - Ensure round-trip: write TOON → read TOON → write TOON is byte-identical

2. Update command implementations
   - cmd/bdt/create.go: Write newly created issues to TOON format
   - cmd/bdt/list.go: Read issues from TOON format
   - cmd/bdt/show.go: Read from TOON format
   - cmd/bdt/import.go: Convert imported JSONL to TOON
   - cmd/bdt/export.go: Export TOON to JSONL (for bd compatibility)

3. Implement TOON parser that handles gotoon's encoder-only limitation
   - Since gotoon doesn't decode TOON, need custom TOON→JSON decoder
   - OR continue storing TOON but decoding via intermediate JSON conversion

4. Git merge driver optimization
   - TOON is line-oriented, better for 3-way merges than binary formats
   - Configure git merge driver for .toon files

5. Comprehensive testing
   - Round-trip tests: Issue → TOON → storage → read → Issue
   - Merge conflict resolution tests with TOON format
   - Large issue set performance tests

## Success Criteria
- issues.toon stores actual TOON format (not JSONL)
- bdt list reads from TOON file
- bdt create writes to TOON file
- Round-trip: create issue → list → show returns identical data
- All 65+ tests still passing
- Performance comparable to JSONL storage","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:05:41.394964404-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T14:37:17.879612634-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T14:37:17.879612634-07:00"}

 {"id":"bd-indn","title":"bd template commands fail with daemon mode","description":"The `bd template show` and `bd template instantiate` commands fail with 'Error loading template: no database connection' when daemon is running.

**Reproduction:**
```bash
bd daemon --start
bd template show bd-qqc  # Error: no database connection
bd template show bd-qqc --no-daemon  # Works
```

**Expected:** Template commands should work with daemon like other commands.

**Workaround:** Use `--no-daemon` flag.

**Location:** Likely in cmd/bd/template.go - daemon RPC path not implemented for template operations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T22:57:35.16596-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T22:57:35.16596-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-ykd9","title":"Add bd doctor --fix flag to automatically repair issues","description":"Implement a --fix flag for bd doctor that can automatically repair detected issues.

Requirements:
- Add --fix flag to bd doctor command
- Show all fixable issues and prompt for confirmation before applying fixes
- Organize fix implementations under doctor/fix/<type_of_fix>.go
- Each fix type should have its own file (e.g., doctor/fix/hooks.go, doctor/fix/sync.go)
- Display what will be fixed and ask user to confirm (Y/n) before proceeding
- Support fixing issues like:
  - Missing or broken git hooks
  - Sync problems with remote
  - File permission issues
  - Any other auto-repairable issues doctor detects

Implementation notes:
- Maintain separation between detection (existing doctor code) and repair (new fix code)
- Each fix should be idempotent and safe to run multiple times
- Provide clear output about what was fixed
- Log any fixes that fail with actionable error messages","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-14T18:17:48.411264-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-14T18:17:58.88609-08:00"}

## Deliverables

 {"id":"bd-z86n","title":"Code Review: PR #551 - Persist close_reason to issues table","description":"Code review of PR #551 which fixes close_reason persistence bug.

## Summary
The PR correctly fixes a bug where close_reason was only stored in the events table, not in the issues.close_reason column. This caused `bd show --json` to return empty close_reason.

## What Was Fixed
- ✅ CloseIssue now updates both close_reason and closed_at
- ✅ ReOpenIssue clears both close_reason and closed_at
- ✅ Comprehensive tests added for both storage and CLI layers
- ✅ Clear documentation in queries.go about dual storage strategy

## Quality Assessment
✅ Tests cover both storage layer and CLI JSON output
✅ Handles reopen case (clearing close_reason)
✅ Good comments explaining dual-storage design
✅ No known issues

## Potential Followups
See linked issues for suggestions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:06.887069-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:06.887069-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-z8a6","title":"bd delete --from-file should add deleted issues to deletions manifest","description":"When using bd delete --from-file to bulk delete issues, the deleted issue IDs are not being added to the deletions.jsonl manifest.

This causes those issues to be resurrected during bd sync when git history scanning finds them in old commits.

Expected: All deleted issues should be added to deletions.jsonl so they wont be reimported from git history.

Workaround: Manually add deletion records to deletions.jsonl.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:48:14.099855-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
